hero.tagline: I design and run cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, and observability so teams deliver software that stays reliable in production.

about.title: About me
about.bio_1: I'm Raúl Rivero Matesanz — a DevOps & Cloud Engineer with almost 5 years building and operating production systems on AWS, Azure, and GCP. Software Engineering graduate (UPM) with international experience (Hanze University, Groningen). I specialize in reliability, automation, and infrastructure as code: pipelines, Terraform, containers, and observability that keep engineering teams unblocked. I communicate clearly across technical and business stakeholders and gravitate toward technical leadership and architecture decisions.
about.bio_2: Based in Madrid. English C1 (PTE Academic) · Spanish native — effective in international, English-first environments.
about.interests_value: Live music · Fantasy literature · Calisthenics · Volunteering

value.title: What I bring
value.point_1: Hands-on multi-cloud delivery: AWS, Azure, and GCP with Terraform, containers, and infrastructure automation.
value.point_2: CI/CD ownership across GitLab, GitHub, Bitbucket, and Jenkins — pipelines aligned to how the team actually ships.
value.point_3: Observability and reliability: metrics, logs, and dashboards that reduce time to detect and diagnose production issues.
value.point_4: Strong communication: translating incidents, risk, and trade-offs into priorities both sides can act on.
value.point_5: Systems thinking: balancing speed, security, cost, and maintainability before changing critical paths.
value.point_6: Leadership trajectory: DevOps Manager today; former Scrum Master in a fast-moving startup — delivery discipline plus technical depth.

value_delivery.title: Shipping value early
value_delivery.body: I favour small, frequent releases that unblock others: less waiting, real feedback sooner, and less batch risk.

observability.title: Observability
observability.body: Without visibility there is no sustainable improvement: metrics, logs, and traces used well cut diagnosis time and reduce blind firefighting.

iteration.title: Iteration and continuous improvement
iteration.body: Each cycle should leave the system slightly better: automate repetition, document gaps, and refine process with what production teaches.

testing.title: Testing
testing.body: Tests are not optional extras—they are the safety net that lets you change with confidence, from pipelines to checks that protect what matters.

documentation.title: Documentation
documentation.body: What is not written is forgotten or trapped in one person. Short, up-to-date, discoverable docs are part of the product, not an afterthought.

judgment.title: Judgment and tooling
judgment.body: I evaluate new tools with the same rigor as a production change: cost, time, vendor lock-in, and privacy. I don't adopt trends for their own sake; what matters is maintainability and clear boundaries for sensitive data.
